title: Zombicide Black Plague: Friends and Foes and No Rest for the Wicked
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hsiq1QlJqCs

Daniel (@dungeondive) takes a quick look at two expansions for **Zombicide: Black Plague** — *Friends and Foes* and *No Rest for the Wicked* — both also compatible with Green Horde. He's candid that he likes Black Plague more than the original Zombicide without quite loving it, and hopes these boxes nudge him closer.

*Friends and Foes* brings four new survivors, magic familiars, nineteen equipment cards, a tainted abomination and ten new quests, plus five tiles for building your own maps. *No Rest for the Wicked* is the darker haul: zombie rats, ghostly spectral walkers vulnerable only to specific weapons, a ballista siege engine, and a showstopper necromantic dragon mini that stomps across tiles destroying terrain and vomiting zombies onto your heroes.

Along the way he admits his real wish for the series: just one more layer of narrative in the missions — a small deck of event cards or tile-specific rules that would make the gorgeous art *mean* something mechanically. He also shows off three minis he painted (the game that pulled him back into the hobby after a 20-year break) and grumbles that the boxes are far bigger than their contents need.

----

Daniel keeps wishing Zombicide's beautiful tiles did something in play beyond marking walls. When a game's art is this good but purely decorative, does it bother you — or is atmosphere enough?
